zh.lifeSummary: 當Barisone II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea於大約1125年出生在義大利薩德尼亞奧利斯塔諾時，他的父親Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea46歲，母親Hélène de Orru22歲。他和Pellegrina de Lacon至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。他於1164年登記服兵役。他去世於1185年在他的家鄉享年61歲。

zh-hans.Arborea, Oristano, Sardegna, Italy: 意大利薩德尼亞奧利斯塔諾阿波亞

zh-hans.Oristano, Sardinia, Italy: 意大利薩德尼亞奧利斯塔諾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Barisone II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea于大约1125年出生在意大利薩德尼亞奧利斯塔諾时，他的父亲Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea46岁，母亲Hélène de Orru22岁。他和Pellegrina de Lacon至少有3个儿子和1个女儿。他于1164年登记服兵役。他去世于1185年在他的家乡享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lacon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: locative name from Lacon in Wem (Shrops). This name has probably been confused with or absorbed by   Lakin   (1), especially in counties adjoining Shrops, such as Staffs.

2 English: while the name in Yorks could be that in (1) through migration, its appearance in late 14th-century Clint in the north-west of WR Yorks, and the persistent final -can in most early forms of the surname, suggest that it may have had a different but unknown origin.

variants.name: Laken
meanings: 1  English:  see  Lakin  .
2  English:  see  Lacon  .

variants.name: Laking
meanings: English:  see  Lacon   and   Lakin  .

variants.name: Lakin
meanings: Americanized form of Jewish Leykin (from Belarus), a metronymic from Leyke, a pet form of the Yiddish female personal name Leye, from the Hebrew female personal name Lea, from which English Leah is derived (see Genesis 29: 16) + the Slavic possessive suffix -in.
English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Lawkin, a diminutive of Law- (a pet form of the personal name Laurence; see Lawrence ). Compare Law 1 and Larkin .

nameMeanings.name: Serra,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Eduardo, Francisco, Jorge, Mario, Enrique, Jaime, Luis, Manuel, Miguel, Ramon.  Italian Antonio, Angelo, Enrico, Guido, Salvatore, Amedeo, Elio, Marco, Nicolo, Sandro, Caesar, Carlo.  Portuguese Henrique, Joaquim.

Italian, Portuguese, and Catalan: topographic name from Italian, Portuguese, Catalan serra ‘ridge or chain of hills; mountain range’ (from Latin serra ‘saw’), or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, in Italy e.g. Serra d'Aniello and Serra Pedace (Cosenza), Serra San Bruno (Vibo Valentia), and Serracapriola (Foggia), in Valencia and Catalonia (Spain) e.g. Serra d'Almos or Serra d'en Galceran.
Catalan (Serrà): topographic name for someone who lived by a sierra, from Catalan serrà, an adjective derived from serra (see 1 above), the same as Castilian Serrano .

variants.name: Serritella
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Amelio, Angelo, Carmela, Rocco, Romeo, Santa.
Italian: possibly a topographic name related to   Serra  .

variants.name: Sera
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Armando, Fernando, Jose, Alberto, Alvaro, Ana, Assunta, Elia, Enrique, Ernesto, Evelio.  Japanese Yoshio, Hideo, Hitomi, Katsumi, Kenji, Kimihiro, Masao, Mikio, Nobuhiro, Sadao, Takeo, Tsuneo.
Portuguese, Galician, and Catalan: most probably a variant of   Serra  .
Italian (Séra): from a short form of a personal name with this ending, such as Baldissera, Bonasera or Serafino .

variants.name: Serret
meanings: Catalan and southern French: topographic name from a diminutive of Catalan   Serra   and Occitan   Serre  .

variants.name: Serrano
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Pedro, Carlos, Miguel, Francisco, Jesus, Jorge, Ricardo, Rafael.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Wenseslao.

Spanish: topographic name for someone who lived by a mountain ridge or chain of hills, from an adjectival derivative of serra (see Serra ). This surname is also found in the Philippines, Portugal, and Brazil.
Italian (in Calabria Serranò): habitational name for someone from any of various places called Serra (see Serra ). The surname Serrano may also be of Spanish origin.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Barisone II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea was born about 1125, in Oristano, Sardinia, Italy, his father, Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea, was 46 and his mother, Hélène de Orru, was 22. He had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with Pellegrina de Lacon. He registered for military service in 1164. He died in 1185, in his hometown, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Barisone II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea est né environ 1125, à Oristano, Sardegna, Italie, son père, Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Hélène de Orru, avait 22 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec Pellegrina de Lacon. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1164. Il est décédé en 1185, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Barisone II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea um 1125 in Oristano, Sardinien, Italien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea 46 und seine Mutter Hélène de Orru 22. Er hatte mit Pellegrina de Lacon mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er hat sich 1164 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb 1185 im Alter von 61 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Barisone II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea è nato il circa 1125 nel Oristano, Sardegna, Italia, il suo padre, Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea, aveva 46 anni e il suo madre, Hélène de Orru, aveva 22 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia con Pellegrina de Lacon. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1164. Morì nel 1185, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 61 anni.

ja.Genova, Liguria, Italy: イタリアリグリアジェノヴァ
ja.Arborea, Oristano, Sardegna, Italy: イタリアサルデグナオリスタノアルボアア
ja.Arborea, Italy: イタリアサルデグナアルボアア
ja.Oristano, Sardinia, Italy: イタリアサルデグナオリスタノ
ja.lifeSummary: Barisone II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arboreaが約1125年にイタリアサルデグナオリスタノで生まれた時，父Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arboreaは46歳，母Hélène de Orruは22歳であった。彼はPellegrina de Laconとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1164年に兵役登録をした。彼は1185年に故郷で61歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Barisone II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea(이)가 대략 1125년 이탈리아 사르데그나 오리스탠오에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea(은)는 46세였고, 그의 어머니 Hélène de Orru(은)는 22세였다. 그는 Pellegrina de Lacon(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1164년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1185년 그의 고향에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Barisone II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea nasceu aproximadamente 1125, em Oristano, Sardegna, Itália, seu pai, Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, Hélène de Orru, tinha 22 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha com Pellegrina de Lacon. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1164. Ele faleceu em 1185, em sua cidade natal, com 61 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Barisone II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea родился около 1125 г. в местности ористано, сардегна, Италия, его отцу по имени Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Hélène de Orru, 22. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь от Pellegrina de Lacon. В 1164 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер в 1185 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 61 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Barisone II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea nació aproximadamente 1125, en Oristano, Sardegna, Italia, su papá, Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea, tenía 46 años y su mamá, Hélène de Orru, tenía 22 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija con Pellegrina de Lacon. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1164. Murió en 1185, en su pueblo, a la edad de 61 años.
